subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, ECCB, Freedom House, RSF, TI, InSight Crime, ReliefWeb) + international and regional press.

A landmark peaceful change of government (Nov 2025) ended Ralph Gonsalves' 24-year tenure - Godwin Friday's NDP won 14-1 - on the East Caribbean dollar (XCD, pegged 2.70/USD); notably one of the few CBI holdouts (no golden-passport program).

Standout features: strong democratic resilience and the cohort's best CPI, against the highest debt burden of the OECS (~113%), a serious violent-crime problem, and repeat disasters (the 2021 La Soufriere eruption, 2024 Beryl).

economic.notes: Peg-anchored low inflation but the heaviest OECS debt burden and no CBI cushion.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~113% of GDP in 2025 (up ~45 ppts since 2019), 'high risk of distress' but sustainable only if consolidation is fully implemented; no CBI cushion. (IMF 2024 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An ECCB member with a credible peg and no own policy. (ECCB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The XCD is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and stable under the peg, eased post-2023. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: The ECCU system is liquid/well-capitalized; small-system concentration risk. (ECCB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A tiny price-taker with a faded banana legacy and a narrow base, with no leverage. (observed)

institutional.notes: The cohort's best CPI and functioning judiciary, with a cybercrime/defamation-law caveat.

rule_of_law.notes: A functioning judiciary - CPI score 63, rank ~32/180, the best of the cohort. (TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Full IMF/ECCB engagement, with thin capacity. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Small; the new government will test continuity. (observed)

press_freedom.notes: 'Free' but RSF flags a 2016 cybercrime/criminal-defamation law and political media influence. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Exceptional democratic resilience (a landmark 2025 alternation), against rising gun violence.

political_stability.notes: Lifted by the orderly Nov 2025 transfer (NDP 14-1, ending Gonsalves' 24-year tenure), with minor near-term transition uncertainty under new PM Friday. (Newsday; CNW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systemic discrimination reported, with thin data. (lower confidence)

social_cohesion.notes: Generally cohesive, but rising gang/gun violence and disaster displacement strain it. (observed)

demographics.notes: The IMF flags a rapidly aging population as a structural headwind. (IMF)

geopolitical.notes: CARICOM/OECS/ALBA-tied and conflict-free, with less golden-passport exposure than peers.

alliance_reliability.notes: CARICOM/OECS/ALBA ties plus Taiwan-funded projects - predictable but small. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No CBI program means less golden-passport sanction exposure than peers, with standard AML/de-risking pressure. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: Multi-hazard exposure (volcano + hurricanes) and a high homicide rate.

climate.notes: 2024 Hurricane Beryl devastated Union Island/the Grenadines (~90% of structures damaged) - acute SIDS exposure. (IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Basic, with a new Taiwan-funded hospital under construction and off-island referral for serious cases. (observed)

infrastructure.notes: New port and hospital investments are positives; volcanic/storm-damaged areas still recovering. (IMF)

crime_safety.notes: ~54 homicides in 2024 (a rate among the world's highest), with a rising gang/gun culture. (InSight Crime; St Vincent Times)

disaster_insurance.notes: A CCRIF member, but coverage is a small fraction of losses; back-to-back disasters expose the protection gap. (CCRIF)

summary: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines carries the heaviest debt load of the OECS cohort (~113% of GDP) and a violent-crime problem with one of the world's highest per-capita homicide rates, and it has absorbed two major disasters in three years (the 2021 La Soufriere eruption, 2024 Hurricane Beryl). Yet it just demonstrated exceptional democratic resilience: the November 2025 landslide that ended Ralph Gonsalves' 24-year tenure and installed Godwin Friday's NDP was peaceful and orderly, removing the prior dominant-PM risk. Uniquely among CBI-eligible peers it runs no golden-passport program, so it forgoes that revenue but also avoids EU passport-sanction exposure. Net skew is roughly neutral - institutional strength and the new government's reform mandate offset against high debt, crime, and severe climate exposure.
